notable_pick_trades: Acquired 3rd-round pick from Eagles (Jaelan Phillips trade), acquired 3rd-round pick from Texans (2025 draft trade). Have five top-100 selections. Missing 6th-round pick (Darren Waller trade).
roster_context: Full-scale rebuild under new GM Jon-Eric Sullivan and HC Jeff Hafley, both hired from Green Bay. The Dolphins went 7-10 in 2025, fired Mike McDaniel, and have gutted their veteran roster. Tyreek Hill was released (failed physical after knee dislocation), Bradley Chubb released, Jaelan Phillips traded mid-season, James Daniels released, and Tua Tagovailoa is expected to be designated a post-June 1 release with a $99.2M dead cap hit. Minkah Fitzpatrick is being actively shopped. Miami has ~35 pending free agents, roughly $4M in cap space, and is pursuing Malik Willis in free agency as a potential bridge QB. Sullivan has identified Jaylen Waddle, De'Von Achane, Aaron Brewer, Jordyn Brooks, and Patrick Paul as core building blocks. This is a gap year — the team will compete but expects growing pains and is building toward 2027.

needs.explanation: The cornerback room is being gutted. Rasul Douglas, Jack Jones, Kader Kohou, A.J. Green III, and Artie Burns are all pending UFAs. The remaining CBs under contract are second-year Jason Marshall Jr. (5th-round pick), JuJu Brents (coming off season-ending injury), Storm Duck (coming off injury), and fringe roster players. Sullivan mentioned Marshall and Brents as players he's excited about, but the room desperately needs a high-end starter. Miami has been linked to CB prospects at the Combine and in mock drafts consistently.
needs.current_starter: Jason Marshall Jr., Storm Duck, JuJu Brents (all unproven/returning from injury)
needs.depth: Ethan Bonner (tendered ERFA), Ethan Robinson, Isaiah Johnson, Jason Maitre — all practice squad/fringe players

needs.explanation: This position has been decimated. Bradley Chubb was released, Jaelan Phillips was traded to the Eagles mid-season, and Matthew Judon was cut in December. Chop Robinson (2024 1st-round pick) is the only viable edge rusher remaining. Sullivan identified Robinson as a building block, but he's still developing and his snap count actually went down from his rookie year. Miami needs at least one more starting-caliber edge rusher to pair with Robinson, but the investment at EDGE should not come at the expense of higher-priority needs given Robinson's potential.
needs.depth: Essentially none. Quinton Bell and Cameron Goode are UFAs. Khalil Mack (UFA from offseason, exploring options) is not returning to Miami.

needs.explanation: Minkah Fitzpatrick is being actively shopped in trade talks and likely to be moved for a mid-round pick. He carries an $18.8M cap hit and doesn't fit the rebuild timeline at age 29. If traded, the only safeties remaining are rookie Dante Trader Jr. (6th-round pick in 2025) and fringe players. Elijah Campbell, Ifeatu Melifonwu, and Ashtyn Davis are all UFAs. Sullivan and Hafley want versatile nickel/safety players like Green Bay used, and the cupboard is essentially bare. Caleb Downs (Ohio State) has been a popular mock to Miami at #11.
needs.current_starter: Minkah Fitzpatrick (likely to be traded), Dante Trader Jr.
needs.depth: Virtually none if Fitzpatrick is moved. Elijah Campbell and Ashtyn Davis are UFAs.

needs.explanation: After releasing Tyreek Hill (knee dislocation, failed physical) and Nick Westbrook-Ikhine, Jaylen Waddle is the only proven receiver on the roster. Malik Washington caught 46 passes but only had 317 yards. Dee Eskridge is a UFA likely to walk. The team signed Terrace Marshall Jr. to a reserve/futures contract. Slowik's Shanahan-style offense needs talented receivers to function, and Miami desperately needs a big-bodied WR2 complement to Waddle's speed.
needs.depth: Tahj Washington (7th-round pick), Terrace Marshall Jr. (futures), Cedric Wilson Jr. (UFA) — extremely thin

needs.explanation: Both guard spots are significant concerns. Jonah Savaiinaea (2025 2nd-round pick) had an awful rookie year with a 14.1 PFF pass-blocking grade, dead last among guards. James Daniels was released after playing just 3 snaps in 2025. Cole Strange was the starting RG but is a UFA. The team released Liam Eichenberg (failed physical). Center Aaron Brewer is locked in and is a building block (All-Pro caliber), but both guard spots need either development from Savaiinaea or outside additions. A guard/tackle-versatile lineman would be ideal.
needs.current_starter: LG: Jonah Savaiinaea, C: Aaron Brewer, RG: TBD (Cole Strange is UFA)
needs.depth: Andrew Meyer (backup C), Larry Borom — very thin at guard

needs.explanation: Patrick Paul emerged as a franchise left tackle in 2025 and is a core building block. Right tackle Austin Jackson has battled injuries (14 games in 2 years) and carries a $15.3M cap hit, though Sullivan indicated he'd like to keep him. Jackson is in the final year of his deal. The team needs insurance/competition at RT, especially given Jackson's injury history and the possibility of moving a drafted OT prospect to guard. Several mocks have Miami drafting Francis Mauigoa (OT/OG) at #11.
needs.current_starter: LT: Patrick Paul (building block), RT: Austin Jackson (injury-prone, final year of deal)
needs.depth: Paper thin. Daniel Brunskill is a UFA. Liam Eichenberg released.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 (could be first-round pick if OL-first approach taken)

needs.explanation: This is the one position group with recent high investment. Kenneth Grant (2025 1st-round pick, #13 overall) anchors the interior. The team also drafted Jordan Phillips and Zeek Biggers in the 2025 draft. Benito Jones and Matthew Butler are depth pieces. Run defense was still a struggle in 2025, but the young talent is in place. Additional depth could help, but this is not a primary draft target.
needs.depth: Jordan Phillips, Zeek Biggers, Matthew Butler — decent young depth

needs.explanation: Jason Sanders (franchise record-holder, 84.6% career FG%) was released. Miami has Riley Patterson on the roster as a UFA who may be brought back, but no kicker has been confirmed for 2026. Creates a new need that wasn't a priority before. Likely addressed in mid-to-late rounds of the draft or via a low-cost veteran signing.

Jordyn Brooks is a core building block and extension candidate. He was AFC Defensive Player of the Week during the 2025 season. However, depth behind him is non-existent. Willie Gay Jr., K.J. Britt, Quinton Bell, and Caleb Johnson are all UFAs. Tyrel Dodson is under contract for 2026 at $3.7M. The linebacker corps needs youth and athleticism, particularly in coverage, to fit Hafley/Duggan's defensive scheme. PFF described the LB corps as in 'desperate need of youth and athleticism.'
needs.depth: Jackson Woodard (under contract). Nearly all backups are UFAs.

needs.explanation: Jake Bailey signed with the Falcons (3yr/$9M). Miami has no current punter on the roster. A replacement is needed but this is lowest-priority given the team's many high-tier needs.

Tua Tagovailoa is expected to be released (post-June 1) after being benched in Week 16 for Quinn Ewers. The only QB under contract is 7th-round rookie Ewers, who showed limited upside. Miami is pursuing Malik Willis in free agency but may be priced out due to massive dead cap. Sullivan has stated they will 'invest in QB every year.' This draft class is widely considered weak at QB, so Miami may take a developmental QB on Day 3 while targeting the loaded 2027 class (Arch Manning, etc.).
needs.current_starter: Quinn Ewers (7th-round pick, under contract through 2028)
needs.depth: Essentially none. Zach Wilson is a UFA. Signed Shane Buechele to a 1-year deal. Terrace Marshall Jr. on futures contract only.
needs.draft_range_target: Day 3 developmental pick likely; franchise QB more likely targeted in 2027 draft

needs.explanation: Jonnu Smith was traded away in 2025. Darren Waller was placed on IR during the season and is an unrestricted free agent — unlikely to return. Greg Dulcich (UFA) had a decent late-season run (26 catches, 335 yards) and Miami may re-sign him cheaply, but he's not a long-term answer. Julian Hill is an RFA. The team signed Zack Kuntz to a futures contract. In Slowik's Shanahan-style offense, a quality TE is important for play-action and 12-personnel sets.
needs.depth: Julian Hill (RFA), Zack Kuntz (futures), possibly Greg Dulcich if re-signed

scheme_fit_notes: Under Hafley (defensive-minded HC) and DC Sean Duggan, Miami will install a Green Bay-style defense emphasizing versatile safeties who can play in the slot/nickel, physical press-man corners, and multiple fronts. OC Bobby Slowik runs a Shanahan-style zone offense requiring athletic OL who can pull and reach, play-action-friendly TEs, and speed at WR. The scheme prioritizes zone-run blocking from OL, making athletic/movement linemen more valuable than pure power players. Sullivan specifically stated they want players who are 'wired the right way' with grit and toughness — expect a premium on physical, high-character players at every position.
